decision.overall_recommendation: VIX gate is open, so new entries are permissible, but this batch is thin (only 2 candidates cleared screening) and both carry mixed fundamental signals rather than clean uptrends — size conservatively and treat the technical pattern as a candidate filter, not a proven edge. AAP's six-quarter EPS beat streak and accelerating recent growth make it the stronger of the two; ADNT's inconsistent quarter-to-quarter income growth and a flagged stop-sanity issue (RR 39:1) warrant extra caution on that name specifically.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: AAP has beaten EPS estimates in 6 consecutive reported quarters (most recently 0.77 vs 0.39 est. in May 2026 and 0.86 vs 0.41 est. in Feb 2026), and the two most recent quarters show outsized net-income/EPS growth (3.0x and ~7.0x). Recent news is mostly bullish (shares jumped 16.4% on the Q1 2026 beat, CEO citing stronger margins ahead), though there was a notable negative episode in August 2025 when shares fell ~9% despite a beat due to a guidance cut and margin concerns — the fundamental trajectory has clearly improved since then.
ranked_picks.rationale: Ranked above ADNT because it has a longer, cleaner beat streak (6 of last 6 quarters vs. ADNT's mixed 2-of-4) and its most recent income-growth figures are strongly positive rather than oscillating, despite one legitimate negative news episode.
flags: Earnings in 31 days (2026-05-21 report cycle already reflected in DaysToEarnings) — no immediate pre-earnings conflict but monitor as holding period extends

PriceVsPOCPct -0.98% — price sits essentially at POC, normal volume support beneath, not a concern

ranked_picks.research_highlight: ADNT's income growth is volatile quarter to quarter: strong Q2 2026 (+6.1% revenue, EPS +221%) followed a negative Q1 2026 (-1.2% revenue, EPS -227%) and a negative Q4 2025, so there's no clean trend. Earnings history shows a mixed beat/miss pattern — beat in the last 2 reported quarters (0.52 vs 0.37, 0.35 vs 0.184) but missed the 2 quarters before that (0.52 vs 0.55, 0.45 vs 0.47); news is dominated by routine analyst-coverage pieces with one clearly negative item (a Nov 2025 miss described as 'demand volatility' hitting performance).
ranked_picks.rationale: Placed below AAP due to inconsistent income growth and a mixed (not clean) beat record; the extremely wide 39:1 RR is flagged as likely reflecting an unusually tight stop rather than an exceptional target, which should temper conviction on the risk/reward math alone.
flags: StopSanityFlag: true — RR of 39.28:1 is far above typical thresholds, more likely explained by an unusually tight stop (0.36 from entry) than an unusually strong target; verify stop placement before sizing up
Earnings in 23 days — closer to the earnings-buffer edge than AAP, worth confirming buffer logic still holds if entry is delayed
